translations.descricao: Care transition (ToC) refers to the coordinated process of transferring patients with chronic conditions or post-stroke from the hospital environment to the home and community, aiming to improve participation and quality of life. In developing countries, such as Brazil, ToC for young people with special health needs is essential due to the improvement in survival of chronic diseases in childhood. Recommendations include improving communication, using transition coordinators, and educating patients/caregivers.
translations.sus.indicacao: Care transition recommended for young people with chronic diseases starting in childhood and post-stroke patients for integration into the adult health system.
translations.sus.populacaoAlvo: Young people with special health needs and stroke survivors.
translations.sociedadesMedicas.indicacao: American Congress of Rehabilitation Medicine recommends post-stroke ToC with a focus on community participation. Indian Academy of Pediatrics emphasizes organized ToC for youth with chronic conditions.
translations.epidemiologia.prevalencia: A large proportion of children with chronic diseases survive into adulthood, necessitating ToC.
translations.epidemiologia.incidencia: Incidence of need for ToC increases with improvement in survival of pediatric chronic conditions.
translations.epidemiologia.mortalidade: Inadequate transitions contribute to rehospitalizations and higher mortality in post-stroke patients.
